grambank_features.name: What is the order of numeral and noun in the NP?
grambank_features.gloss: Order of numeral and noun in the noun phrase: 1=Numeral-Noun, 2=Noun-Numeral, 3=both orders occur.

grambank_features.name: What is the pragmatically unmarked order of S and V in intransitive clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Unmarked order of S and V in intransitive clauses: 1=SV, 2=VS, 3=both.

grambank_features.name: Is there a count/mass distinction in interrogative quantifiers?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether interrogative quantifiers show a count/mass distinction ('how many' vs 'how much').

grambank_features.name: Do (nominal) content interrogatives normally or frequently occur in situ?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether content interrogatives normally stay in situ (not fronted).

grambank_features.name: Are there three or more distance contrasts in demonstratives?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether demonstratives make three or more distance contrasts (e.g. near/mid/far).

grambank_features.name: Do demonstratives show an elevation distinction?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether demonstratives encode an elevation distinction (up-here vs down-there).

grambank_features.name: Do demonstratives show a visible-nonvisible distinction?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether demonstratives encode a visible-vs-nonvisible distinction.

grambank_features.name: Are there definite or specific articles?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has a definite or specific article (a grammatical word like English 'the').

grambank_features.name: Do indefinite nominals commonly have indefinite articles?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether indefinite nominals commonly take an indefinite article (like English 'a/an').

grambank_features.name: Are there prenominal articles?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an article, if present, comes before the noun (prenominal).

grambank_features.name: Are there postnominal articles?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an article, if present, comes after the noun (postnominal).

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender distinction in independent 3rd person pronouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether independent 3rd-person pronouns mark a gender distinction (he vs she).

grambank_features.name: Is there a dual or unit augmented form (in addition to plural or augmented) for all person categories in the pronoun system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether every person category has a dual (or unit-augmented) form in addition to plural.

grambank_features.name: Are there demonstrative classifiers?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has demonstrative classifiers.

grambank_features.name: Is there productive morphological plural marking on nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive morphological plural marking on nouns.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender/noun class system where sex is a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the gender/noun-class system uses biological sex as a class-assignment factor.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender/noun class system where shape is a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the gender/noun-class system uses shape as a class-assignment factor.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender/noun class system where animacy is a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the gender/noun-class system uses animacy as a class-assignment factor.

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender/noun class system where plant status is a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the gender/noun-class system uses plant status as a class-assignment factor.

grambank_features.name: Are there numeral classifiers?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has numeral classifiers (a sortal element required when counting).

grambank_features.name: Are there possessive classifiers?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has possessive classifiers.

grambank_features.name: Is the adnominal possessive construction different for alienable and inalienable nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the possessive construction differs for alienable vs inalienable nouns (e.g. 'my dog' vs 'my hand').

grambank_features.name: What is the pragmatically unmarked order of adnominal possessor noun and possessed noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Unmarked order of possessor noun and possessed noun: 1=Possessor-Possessed, 2=Possessed-Possessor, 3=both.

grambank_features.name: Is there productive morphological trial marking on nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive morphological trial marking on nouns.

grambank_features.name: Is there productive morphological paucal marking on nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive morphological paucal marking on nouns ('a few').

grambank_features.name: Can an adnominal property word agree with the noun in gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an adjective-like property word can agree with its noun in gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Can an adnominal demonstrative agree with the noun in gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an adnominal demonstrative can agree with its noun in gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Can an article agree with the noun in gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an article can agree with its noun in gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Can the verb carry a marker of animacy of argument, unrelated to any gender/noun class of the argument visible in the NP domain?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the verb marks the animacy of an argument (independent of NP-level gender/class).

grambank_features.name: Can an article agree with the noun in number?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an article can agree with its noun in number.

grambank_features.name: Is there any productive diminutive marking on the noun (exclude marking by system of nominal classification only)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive diminutive marking on the noun (excluding pure noun-class shift).

grambank_features.name: Is there any productive augmentative marking on the noun (exclude marking by system of nominal classification only)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive augmentative marking on the noun (excluding pure noun-class shift).

grambank_features.name: Is there a gender system where a noun's phonological properties are a factor in class assignment?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether a noun's phonological shape is a factor in gender/class assignment.

grambank_features.name: What is the order of adnominal property word and noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Order of adjective-like property word and noun: 0=not used attributively, 1=Adj-N, 2=N-Adj, 3=both.

grambank_features.name: Can an adnominal numeral agree with the noun in gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether an adnominal numeral can agree with its noun in gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked by a non-inflecting word ("auxiliary particle")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked by a non-inflecting negative particle.

grambank_features.name: Are there special adnominal possessive pronouns that are not formed by an otherwise regular process?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there are special adnominal possessive pronouns not formed by a regular process.

grambank_features.name: Can augmentative meaning be expressed productively by a shift of gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether augmentative meaning can be expressed by shifting gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Can diminutive meaning be expressed productively by a shift of gender/noun class?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether diminutive meaning can be expressed by shifting gender/noun class.

grambank_features.name: Is trial number regularly marked in the noun phrase by a dedicated phonologically free element?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether trial number is marked in the NP by a dedicated free (unbound) element.

grambank_features.name: Is there a large class of nouns whose gender/noun class is not phonologically or semantically predictable?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a large class of nouns whose gender/class is not phonologically or semantically predictable.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a prefix on the possessor?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a prefix on the possessor.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a prefix on the possessed noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a prefix on the possessed noun.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a suffix on the possessor?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a suffix on the possessor.

grambank_features.name: Can adnominal possession be marked by a suffix on the possessed noun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether adnominal possession can be marked by a suffix on the possessed noun.

grambank_features.name: Can tense be marked by a non-inflecting word ("auxiliary particle")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether tense can be marked by a non-inflecting particle.

grambank_features.name: Is there a decimal numeral system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the numeral system is decimal (base 10).

grambank_features.name: Is there synchronic evidence for any element of a quinary numeral system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is any synchronic evidence of a quinary (base 5) element.

grambank_features.name: Is there synchronic evidence for any element of a vigesimal numeral system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is any synchronic evidence of a vigesimal (base 20) element.

grambank_features.name: Is there a body-part tallying system?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a body-part tallying counting system.

grambank_features.name: Is there productive overt morphological singular marking on nouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is productive overt singular marking on nouns.

grambank_features.name: Are there morphological cases for oblique non-pronominal NPs (i.e. not S/A/P)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether oblique (non-core) non-pronominal NPs carry morphological case.

grambank_features.name: Are there prepositions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has prepositions.

grambank_features.name: Are there postpositions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has postpositions.

grambank_features.name: Are variations in marking strategies of core participants based on TAM distinctions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether core-argument marking varies by TAM (tense/aspect/mood) — e.g. split by tense.

grambank_features.name: Are variations in marking strategies of core participants based on person distinctions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether core-argument marking varies by person (e.g. a person-based split in ergativity).

grambank_features.name: Can the recipient in a ditransitive construction be marked like the monotransitive patient?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the recipient of a ditransitive ('give') can be marked like a monotransitive object.

grambank_features.name: Is singular number regularly marked in the noun phrase by a dedicated phonologically free element?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether singular number is marked in the NP by a dedicated free (unbound) element.

grambank_features.name: Is there any accusative alignment of flagging?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether any part of the case/adposition (flagging) system shows accusative alignment.

grambank_features.name: Is there any ergative alignment of flagging?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether any part of the case/adposition (flagging) system shows ergative alignment.

grambank_features.name: Is there any neutral alignment of flagging?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether any part of the case/adposition (flagging) system shows neutral alignment (S=A=P unmarked).

grambank_features.name: Is there a politeness distinction in 2nd person forms?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a politeness (T/V) distinction in 2nd-person forms.

grambank_features.name: Is there a distinction between inclusive and exclusive?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the pronoun system distinguishes inclusive ('we incl. you') from exclusive ('we excl. you').

grambank_features.name: Is there a male/female distinction in 2nd person independent pronouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether 2nd-person independent pronouns mark a male/female distinction.

grambank_features.name: Is there a male/female distinction in 1st person independent pronouns?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether 1st-person independent pronouns mark a male/female distinction.

grambank_features.name: Is there a phonologically independent reflexive pronoun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a phonologically independent reflexive pronoun.

grambank_features.name: Is there a phonologically independent non-bipartite reciprocal pronoun?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a phonologically independent (non-bipartite) reciprocal pronoun.

grambank_features.name: Do core adjectives (defined semantically as property concepts such as value, shape, age, dimension) act like verbs in predicative position?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether core adjectives behave like verbs when used as a predicate ('the dog is big' patterns like 'the dog runs').

grambank_features.name: Are there morphological cases for non-pronominal core arguments (i.e. S/A/P)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether non-pronominal core arguments (S/A/P) carry morphological case.

grambank_features.name: Do verbs have prefixes/proclitics, other than those that only mark A, S or P (do include portmanteau: A & S + TAM)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verbs take prefixes/proclitics beyond pure S/A/P agreement (incl. portmanteau agreement+TAM).

grambank_features.name: Do verbs have suffixes/enclitics, other than those that only mark A, S or P (do include portmanteau: A & S + TAM)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verbs take suffixes/enclitics beyond pure S/A/P agreement (incl. portmanteau agreement+TAM).

grambank_features.name: Is there productive infixation in verbs?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verbs show productive infixation (a morpheme inserted inside the root).

grambank_features.name: Is there overt morphological marking of present tense on verbs?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether present tense is overtly marked on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there overt morphological marking on the verb dedicated to past tense?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a dedicated overt past-tense marker on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there overt morphological marking on the verb dedicated to future tense?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a dedicated overt future-tense marker on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Can the S argument be indexed by a suffix/enclitic on the verb in the simple main clause?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the S (intransitive subject) can be indexed by a suffix/enclitic on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Can the S argument be indexed by a prefix/proclitic on the verb in the simple main clause?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the S (intransitive subject) can be indexed by a prefix/proclitic on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Can the A argument be indexed by a suffix/enclitic on the verb in the simple main clause?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the A (transitive subject) can be indexed by a suffix/enclitic on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Can the A argument be indexed by a prefix/proclitic on the verb in the simple main clause?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the A (transitive subject) can be indexed by a prefix/proclitic on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Can the P argument be indexed by a suffix/enclitic on the verb in the simple main clause?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the P (transitive object) can be indexed by a suffix/enclitic on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Can the P argument be indexed by a prefix/proclitic on the verb in the simple main clause?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the P (transitive object) can be indexed by a prefix/proclitic on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Are variations in marking strategies of core participants based on verb classes?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether core-argument marking varies by verb class.

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked by an affix, clitic or modification of the verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked by an affix, clitic, or verb modification.

grambank_features.name: Is there verb suppletion for participant number?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verbs are suppletive for participant number (different root for singular vs plural participant).

grambank_features.name: Are there conjugation classes?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verbs fall into conjugation classes.

grambank_features.name: Is there a phonologically bound reflexive marker on the verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a bound (affixal) reflexive marker on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there a phonologically bound reciprocal marker on the verb?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a bound (affixal) reciprocal marker on the verb.

grambank_features.name: Do verbs classify the shape, size or consistency of absolutive arguments by means of incorporated nouns, verbal affixes or suppletive verb stems?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether verbs classify the shape/size/consistency of absolutive arguments (e.g. via incorporated nouns or suppletion).

grambank_features.name: Can tense be marked by an inflecting word ("auxiliary verb")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether tense can be marked by an inflecting auxiliary verb.

grambank_features.name: Is there a notably small number, i.e. about 100 or less, of verb roots in the language?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the language has a small closed set of verb roots (about 100 or fewer).

grambank_features.name: Is a pragmatically unmarked constituent order verb-initial for transitive clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the unmarked transitive constituent order is verb-initial.

grambank_features.name: Is a pragmatically unmarked constituent order verb-medial for transitive clauses?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the unmarked transitive constituent order is verb-medial.

grambank_features.name: Is the order of core argument (i.e. S/A/P) constituents fixed?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether the order of core (S/A/P) constituents is fixed.

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked clause-finally?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked clause-finally.

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked clause-initially?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked clause-initially.

grambank_features.name: Is there a difference between imperative (prohibitive) and declarative negation constructions?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether prohibitive (negative imperative) negation differs from declarative negation.

grambank_features.name: Can standard negation be marked by an inflecting word ("auxiliary verb")?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether standard negation can be marked by an inflecting negative auxiliary verb.

grambank_features.name: Are there multiple past or multiple future tenses, distinguishing distance from Time of Reference?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there are multiple past or future tenses distinguishing distance in time (e.g. recent vs remote past).

grambank_features.name: Is there an interrogative verb for content interrogatives (who?, what?, etc.)?
grambank_features.gloss: Whether there is a dedicated interrogative verb for content questions ('do-what?').

aus_extension.name: Case marked separately on pronouns vs nouns
aus_extension.gloss: Whether both non-pronominal core arguments and pronominal core arguments carry case (so case can differ by word class).

aus_extension.name: A argument indexed on the verb
aus_extension.gloss: Whether the transitive subject (A) is cross-referenced by a bound marker on the verb.

aus_extension.name: P argument indexed on the verb
aus_extension.gloss: Whether the transitive object (P) is cross-referenced by a bound marker on the verb.

aus_extension.name: Nominal paucal number
aus_extension.gloss: Productive morphological paucal ('a few') on nouns.

aus_extension.name: Nominal trial number
aus_extension.gloss: Productive morphological trial (exactly three) on nouns.

aus_extension.name: Distance contrasts in demonstratives
aus_extension.gloss: Number of distance contrasts (2, 3+ deictic terms).

aus_extension.name: Verb conjugation classes
aus_extension.gloss: Whether verbs fall into conjugation classes (as in most Pama-Nyungan languages).

aus_extension.name: Multiple (distance) past/future tenses
aus_extension.gloss: Whether there are multiple past or future tenses distinguishing temporal distance (e.g. recent vs remote past) — common in Australian languages.

aus_extension.name: Verb suppletion for participant number
aus_extension.gloss: Whether a verb uses a different root for singular vs plural participant (e.g. 'go.sg' vs 'go.pl').

aus_extension.name: Bound reflexive marker on verb
aus_extension.gloss: A phonologically bound reflexive marker on the verb (e.g. Kuku Yalanji -ji-).

aus_extension.name: Bound reciprocal marker on verb
aus_extension.gloss: A phonologically bound reciprocal marker on the verb (e.g. Kuku Yalanji -wa-).

aus_extension.name: Prohibitive vs declarative negation
aus_extension.gloss: Whether the prohibitive (negative imperative) uses a different construction from declarative negation.

aus_extension.name: Numeral base system
aus_extension.gloss: Base of the numeral system: decimal, quinary, vigesimal, body-part tally, or restricted (many Australian languages have very small numeral systems).
